Definition: [In this section an overview will be given of the used terms and definition specifically of this product.]
Service Summary: The Cegeka Digital Identity Services offers operational support and guidance by subject matter experts to ensure that the Identity and Access Management (IAM) solution is maintained, secured, and remains compliant with current regulations. A typical IAM solution is a family of cloud identity and network access products. It enables organizations to implement a Zero Trust security strategy and create a trust fabric that verifies identities, validates access conditions, checks permissions, encrypts connection channels, and monitors for compromise. An IAM solution consists of the following capabilities which will be supported as part of this service: This service helps the customer to build a cybersecurity resilient environment by controlling key areas as required by multiple regulations. The Zero Trust security strategy includes these key principles: The Digital Identity Services service provided by Cegeka maintains the customer's IAM solution and offers proactive recommendations for improvements. Additionally, Cegeka will provide usage insights through a dashboard that contains all essential information, giving the customer a comprehensive overview of the service's usage.
Standard Services: The following base services will be delivered as part of the Digital Identity Services service: Evaluate the Identity Secure Score and deliver monthly advice on Security topics to achieve a score which is above typical company average provided by Microsoft. Evaluate the IAM solution and the best practice recommendations and deliver quarterly advice on best practice recommendations. Track identity anomalies and deliver monthly advice how to improve. The following anomalies will be monitored as part of this service: Successful & Failed Sign-ins Successful & Failed Self Service Password Resets Legacy authentication Sign-ins IAM Solution MFA Sign-ins failures Inactive Accounts License assignment failures Conditional Access Gaps and Recommendations from Microsoft Provide Dashboard of the Digital Identity Services service to present information about this service including the KPIs. Monitoring on expiration of application secrets and certificates; Track license group assignments deliver monthly advice how to optimize licensing assignments; Managing, track and deliver quarterly advice on External Identities (B2B) capabilities; Monthly check on Lifecycle Management of the Entra Connect software; Technical L2/L3 incident handling As part of the standard services the following functionality within the IAM solution will be supported: Passwordless Authentication (Hello for Business Hybrid)(*); Temporary Access Pass; (*) Full Passwordless Authentication (without any passwords in-use) requires the additional SKU Management of Full Passwordless Identity Solution the be active. (**) For Face check additional licenses and resources are required, these will be described later in this document.
Optional Services: The following base services will be delivered as part of the Digital Identity Services service: Management of the full passwordless configuration and providing monthly advice; Additional Technical L2/L3 incident handling for a full passwordless configuration;

License description/remarks Microsoft Entra ID Plan 1 Required for delivering the standard services Azure Subscription Required for delivering the Face check of Verified ID
Location: This service is not delivered from within the Customer premises. Only from the Cegeka (virtual) Office Environment.
Ownership: All automation used to deliver this service remains the property of Cegeka at all times.

ACTIVITY MATRIX CUSTOMER CEGEKA STANDARD SERVICES MANAGEMENT OF IDENTITY & ACCESS MANAGEMENT SOLUTION Monitor Identity Secure Score and deliver monthly advice on Security topics to achieve a score which is above typical company average provided by Microsoft   Monitor the IAM solution and the best practice recommendations and deliver monthly advice on best practice recommendations   Monitor of identity anomalies and deliver monthly advice how to improve   Provide Dashboard of the Digital Identity Services service   Monitor license group assignments deliver monthly advice how to optimize licensing assignments   Managing, Monitoring and deliver quarterly advice on external identities (B2B) capabilities   Monthly  check on Lifecycle Management of the Entra Connect software;   Technical L2/L3 incident handling   OPTIONAL SERVICE Management of Full Passwordless Identity Solution Management of the full passwordless configuration and providing advice   Additional Technical L2/L3 incident handling for a full passwordless configuration   RACI Role definition = Responsible : The party who is responsible for the execution of the activity. = Accountable : The party ultimately accountable for the correct and thorough completion of the task and/or activity as performed by the Responsible. For each task and/or activity exactly one Accountable must be specified. The Accountable has the mandate to accept/dismiss on work provided by the Responsible. The Accountable is by default reactive. = Consulted : The party who must be consulted and must approve or provide input prior to execution. This party (partly) gives direction to the result. This is about two-way communication. = Informed : That is the person/party who is informed about the decisions, progress and results achieved, so that the next step can be taken. This is about one-way communication.
Onboarding: (Technical) Implementation Phase This document only describes the implementation steps required for this service. The governance activities around the implementation of this service are not part of this approach and need to be added manually based on the customer project. The implementation phase of the project consists of the following: In the following paragraphs these phases will be described in more detail. Initiation & Design This phase begins with a face-to-face session where the customer defines the requirements for the implementation. During this session, Cegeka will present the standard approach for implementing the service and its components. The standard approach includes a standard design with predefined design choices. Based on the requirements discussed in the session, adjustments to the design can be made where allowed by Cegeka's standards. Additionally, it will be determined which optional services will be implemented along with the base service. This phase has the following set of minimal activities: The results of the phase should be the following documents: Technical Design of the service and a list acceptation criteria for the implementation of the service approved by the customer. Implementation & Testing During this phase, the base service will be implemented along with any additional services. Cegeka will perform the implementation based on the designs prepared in the previous phase. The process begins with implementing and testing the base services. This includes enabling a hybrid identity and authentication solution, which will be configured within Microsoft Entra. Once the base services are implemented, validated, and tested, the additional services will be added and configured. Production Rollout The production rollout of this service will consist of syncing all users towards Microsoft Entra ID. From this moment all users will be able to use the Hybrid Identity for authentication against AD and Entra ID connected resources. Authentication will be configured for all users and this service is activated for all users.
